description_long: This object is a vintage television set with a wooden cabinet exterior, featuring a large, rounded rectangular screen framed by a cream-colored bezel. The front panel includes four prominent rotary knobs and a central oval badge, likely for brand identification, situated below the screen. The lower section of the front houses a fabric-covered speaker grille, and the unit stands on four short, angled legs. Both side panels are plain except for a circular array of ventilation holes, suggesting internal heat dissipation. The overall design is boxy with rounded edges, typical of mid-20th-century televisions, and the finish is a polished, dark wood veneer.
description: A vintage wooden television set with a rounded screen, cream bezel, rotary knobs, and a fabric speaker grille, standing on four short legs.
description_view_0: Front view showing the screen, cream bezel, four rotary knobs, central badge, and speaker grille.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying a plain wooden panel with a circular array of ventilation holes.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a smooth, unadorned wooden surface with no visible features.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a matching circular array of ventilation holes.
attribute_bezel_design: Thick, cream-colored, rounded rectangular bezel made of plastic with a smooth finish.
attribute_control_button_layout: Four rotary knobs arranged in a horizontal row below the screen, with a central badge between them.
